weather.tip: Overcast and cool at 21.2°C this morning with only an 8% rain chance today, so you likely won't need an umbrella — but it'll climb to near 32°C by afternoon, so dress in layers for the swing.

around.headline: Delivery agent allegedly barges into Bengaluru woman's home, exposes himself
around.summary: A Bengaluru woman says a delivery agent pushed his way into her home to use the toilet and then behaved inappropriately; police are investigating. Hand off packages at the doorstep only — never let delivery or service staff come inside, whatever excuse is offered.

around.headline: Minor fire breaks out at Nagasandra Metro Station
around.summary: A small fire was reported at Nagasandra Metro Station on the Purple Line; services were reportedly unaffected. Commuters on this stretch should watch Namma Metro's official channels for any updates before heading out.

around.headline: GBA rolls out new toolkit to tackle Bengaluru's parking crunch
around.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority has introduced fresh public parking rules aimed at the city's chronic street-parking chaos. Expect tighter enforcement near markets and commercial stretches in the coming weeks.

around.headline: New direct AC bus links Electronics City to Tumakuru
around.summary: A new AC bus service now runs a direct route between Electronics City and Tumakuru, cutting commute time for tech workers on that corridor. Worth checking BMTC's schedule if this overlaps your route.

around.headline: Illegal flex banners flagged as footpath hazard in Byatarayanapura
around.summary: Unauthorised flex banners are blocking and endangering pedestrians on Byatarayanapura footpaths, a fresh report finds. Obstructions like these can be reported to BBMP/GBA for removal.

around.headline: US-Iran tensions escalate over the Strait of Hormuz
around.summary: The US and Iran are trading threats over shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for roughly a fifth of the world's oil supply. A prolonged standoff there tends to push crude — and eventually pump prices — higher, worth watching if you're budgeting for fuel.
around.source: The Times of India / Al Jazeera

street_smart.title: A delivery agent at your door asks to use your bathroom before handing over the package. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real case reported today: a Bengaluru woman says a delivery agent pushed his way into her home to use the toilet and exposed himself; police are investigating. (NDTV)
street_smart.situation: Your order arrives and the delivery agent, visibly in a hurry, says he desperately needs to use your washroom before handing over the package. He's got a uniform and the delivery app open on his phone. What do you do?

choices.text: Let him in — he's clearly a genuine delivery agent with the app open, and refusing feels rude.
choices.feedback: This mirrors how today's Bengaluru case allegedly unfolded — a visible uniform, an open app, and a request framed as an emergency are exactly the props used to get past a closed door. A badge or app screen confirms he made the delivery; it says nothing about what happens once he's inside your home.

choices.text: Ask him to wait, hand the package through a partially open or chained door, and firmly decline the washroom request.
choices.feedback: Correct. Completing the handoff at the threshold — package out, any OTP or payment confirmed — keeps a stranger from ever crossing into your home. That doorway boundary matters far more than how convincing or urgent the excuse sounds.

choices.text: Suggest he use the building's common washroom or a shop nearby instead of yours.
choices.feedback: Better than letting him in, but negotiating at an open door still keeps you exposed longer than needed. A flat no and a fast handoff is safer than offering alternatives that extend the conversation.

choices.text: Call out loudly for a family member or neighbour first, then decide.
choices.feedback: This can deter bad intent, but it isn't a substitute for the actual boundary — if you're alone, don't open the door wide or step back to let anyone in, regardless of who else you've alerted.

takeaways: Never let a delivery or service person inside your home to use facilities, charge a phone, or 'just check something' — hand off at the door, chained or ajar, every time.
If someone pushes past a closed door or turns aggressive, call 112 immediately and note the delivery company, order ID, and the rider's photo from the app.
Rate or report the rider in the app afterward if anything felt off, even if nothing happened — it flags the account before the next attempt.

quiz.q: According to today's reports, what did a delivery agent allegedly do after a Bengaluru woman opened her door?
options: Delivered the package and left immediately
Asked to use her toilet and then exposed himself
Demanded a tip before handing over the order
quiz.why: Police are investigating the case; safety guidance is to never let delivery staff past the doorstep, regardless of the reason given.

quiz.q: Where in Bengaluru did a minor fire break out at a metro station today?
quiz.why: The fire was reported at Nagasandra Metro Station on the Purple Line; services were reportedly unaffected.

quiz.q: Which strategic waterway is at the center of the escalating US-Iran standoff in today's news?
quiz.why: Roughly a fifth of the world's oil supply passes through the Strait of Hormuz, so tensions there tend to move fuel prices globally, including in India.

quiz.q: Under India's Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005, within how many days must a public authority normally respond to a request?
quiz.why: The standard window is 30 days, but it drops to 48 hours if the request concerns a threat to life or liberty.

quiz.q: Which vitamin does the human body produce when skin is exposed to sunlight?
quiz.why: Vitamin D deficiency is common among people who spend long hours indoors at a desk — a short walk outside helps.
